programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: (Subbed) ARSENE WENGER AND HIS MANAGERIAL JOURNEY
programme.sub-title: We explore Arsène Wenger's managerial journey before joining Arsenal, tracing his evolution from a young, innovative coach in France to a transformative force in European football. Delving into his time at Nancy, AS Monaco and Nagoya Grampus Eight, Wenger's tactical brilliance began to shape his future success. We highlight the key moments that led him to one of football's most iconic roles.
programme.desc: We explore Arsène Wenger's managerial journey before joining Arsenal, tracing his evolution from a young, innovative coach in France to a transformative force in European football. We highlight the key moments that led him to one of football's most iconic roles.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP40: (Subbed) DANIEL MUNOZ
programme.sub-title: He didn’t make it as a professional until he was 20 years old but always dreamt of playing for his boyhood club, Atletico Nacional. Having realised that dream, Daniel Muñoz has enjoyed a brilliant first full season in South London and now has his sights on FA cup glory with Crystal Palace. The Colombian fan’s favourite speaks to The Big Interview this week about his rapid rise to the Premier League and how he’s longing to give Eagles fans their first ever major trophy.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP43: (Subbed) EDUARDO DA SILVA
programme.sub-title: Eduardo da Silva will forever be synonymous for suffering one of the worst injuries in Premier League history. We travelled to Brazil, Croatia and caught up with the former Arsenal hero here in England to find out more about the man who gave Gunners fans a fleeting glimpse of greatness before being so cruelly deprived.
programme.desc: Eduardo da Silva will forever be synonymous for suffering one of the worst injuries in Premier League history. We travelled to Brazil, Croatia and caught up with the former Arsenal hero here in England to find out more about the man who gave Gunners fans a fleeting glimpse of greatness before being so cruelly deprived.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP31: (Subbed) PERVIS ESTUPINAN
programme.sub-title: After a nomadic start to his career, it now seems that Pervis Estupinan has found his footballing home at Brighton & Hove Albion. The Ecuadorian international takes The Big Interview to the beachfront where he talks about influence of a former professional in the family while growing up, how playing in Spain helped him to become the Premier League performer he is today and his excitement for the future playing under Fabian Hurzeler.
programme.desc: After a nomadic start to his career, it now seems that Pervis Estupinan has found his footballing home at Brighton & Hove Albion. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP40: (Subbed) DANIEL MUNOZ
programme.sub-title: He didn’t make it as a professional until he was 20 years old but always dreamt of playing for his boyhood club, Atletico Nacional. Having realised that dream, Daniel Muñoz has enjoyed a brilliant first full season in South London and now has his sights on FA cup glory with Crystal Palace. The Colombian fan’s favourite speaks to The Big Interview this week about his rapid rise to the Premier League and how he’s longing to give Eagles fans their first ever major trophy.
programme.desc: He didn’t make it as a professional until he was 20 years old but always dreamt of playing for his boyhood club, Atletico Nacional. The Colombian fan’s favourite speaks to The Big Interview this week about his rapid rise to the Premier League and how he’s longing to give Eagles fans their first ever major trophy.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP50: CHRIS WOOD
programme.sub-title: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he’s become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.
programme.desc: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. programme.title: PL STORIES EP45: TITLE RUN IN: TRUE TURNING POINTS
programme.sub-title: PL Stories takes a look at the forgotten moments that truly defined a Premier League season, offering new perspectives on the games that shaped title races. As the season nears its conclusion, two former Premier League players alongside two football experts revisit iconic yet often overlooked moments, such as Steven Gerrard’s infamous slip in 2014, which many believe ended Liverpool’s title charge. But could it have actually been the following match, when Liverpool let a three-goal lead slip against Crystal Palace, that sealed their fate? Our experts debate these key moments and challenge the common narratives that have shaped Premier League history, sparking fresh conversation on what really determined the outcome of those unforgettable seasons.
programme.desc: PL Stories takes a look at the forgotten moments that truly defined a Premier League season, offering new perspectives on the games that shaped title races. As the season nears its conclusion, two former Premier League players alongside two football experts revisit iconic yet often overlooked moments, such as Steven Gerrard’s infamous slip in 2014, which many believe ended Liverpool’s title charge. But could it have actually been the following match, when Liverpool let a three-goal lead slip against Crystal Palace, that sealed their fate? Our experts debate these key moments and challenge the common narratives that have shaped Premier League history, sparking fresh conversation on what really determined the outcome of those unforgettable seasons.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP26: THE GREATEST GAME EVER PLAYED
programme.sub-title: We gather a panel of experts to tackle a nearly impossible question: What truly is the Greatest Premier League Game Ever? Join two ex-footballers, a seasoned commentator, and a top football journalist as they lock horns in a passionate roundtable discussion. The panel revisits iconic moments, dissecting tactics, and legendary performances. Expect banter, bold claims, and maybe even a few surprises, as they strive to settle the debate—or perhaps keep it alive. Whether reliving “Fergie Time” magic, Leicester’s miracle season, or Aguero’s legendary finish, this episode celebrates the unfiltered drama that only Premier League history can offer.
programme.desc: We gather a panel of experts to tackle a nearly impossible question: What truly is the Greatest Premier League Game Ever? programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: KICKS AND CULTURE STATESIDE
programme.sub-title: The Premier League is seeing a big boost in popularity amongst the Black American population and we travelled Stateside to learn more about this growing appeal. The show is led by Aaron Dolores, football fan and founder of Black Arrow, a creative movement on a quest to promote the intersection of football and black culture, not just in the US but worldwide. He takes us on a journey where we meet varying characters from the Mayor of Birmingham Alabama, Randall Woodfin, former players Tim Howard and Jozy Altidore, to current player Chris Richards as well as authentic Black American fans immersed in Premier League culture.
programme.desc: The Premier League is seeing a big boost in popularity amongst the Black American population and we travelled Stateside to learn more about this growing appeal. programme.title: PL REWIND EP20: MCI v QPR 11/12
programme.sub-title: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.
programme.desc: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. programme.title: PL STORIES EP48: (Subbed) REGGAE RHYTHMS IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE
programme.sub-title: Reggae Rhythms in the Premier League explores the powerful connection between Jamaica and the world’s most-watched football league, showcasing how the island’s culture, music, and style have influenced the Premier League. We feature iconic Jamaican players who have left their mark, while also diving into the impact of reggae music, fashion, and fan culture on the game. Through exclusive interviews and untold stories, we see how Jamaican heritage has shaped both football and broader global culture, from the pitch to the streets.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.
programme.desc: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. programme.title: PL STORIES EP51: GOODBYE GOODISON
programme.sub-title: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP50: CHRIS WOOD
programme.sub-title: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he’s become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: KICKS AND CULTURE STATESIDE
programme.sub-title: The Premier League is seeing a big boost in popularity amongst the Black American population and we travelled Stateside to learn more about this growing appeal. The show is led by Aaron Dolores, football fan and founder of Black Arrow, a creative movement on a quest to promote the intersection of football and black culture, not just in the US but worldwide. He takes us on a journey where we meet varying characters from the Mayor of Birmingham Alabama, Randall Woodfin, former players Tim Howard and Jozy Altidore, to current player Chris Richards as well as authentic Black American fans immersed in Premier League culture.
programme.desc: The Premier League is seeing a big boost in popularity amongst the Black American population and we travelled Stateside to learn more about this growing appeal. programme.title: PL STORIES EP22: (Subbed) ASMIR BEGOVIC
programme.sub-title: After a turbulent childhood, including leaving his country of birth due to war, Asmir Begović’s determination led to him to a Premier League winners medal. The 37 year-old goalkeeper talks us through his journey from Bosnia to Everton with many stops in between.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP07: BRAD FRIEDEL
programme.sub-title: Arguably the greatest American goalkeeper of all time, Brad Friedel made an incredible 450 appearances in the Premier League playing for Tottenham, Aston Villa, Blackburn and Liverpool. We sit down with the 52-year-old to recount his incredible journey to the top of English football.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP41: MORATO
programme.sub-title: “Beautiful!” was the reaction from Morato when he first set eyes on The City Ground. Having joined Nottingham Forest last summer, the Brazilian sits down with The Big Interview in the final week of what’s been a remarkable season. The 23-year-old shares his thoughts about Forest’s fortunes under Nuno Espirito Santo, his career journey to England and how losing his sister to cancer when she was just 18 has impacted his life.

programme.title: CLASSIC MATCH SHORTS 15/16 - SOU 4-2 MCI
programme.sub-title: We delve into the Premier League archive to bring you a Classic Match from the 1999/2000 season.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP20: MCI v QPR 11/12
programme.sub-title: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.
programme.desc: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP47: (Subbed) MO SALAH
programme.sub-title: We look back on the extraordinary career of Mohamed Salah, tracing his rise from a small village in Egypt to global football stardom. We speak to those closest to him to highlight his early years, struggles, and determination, shedding light on the pivotal moments that shaped his journey to becoming a Premier League legend with Liverpool.
programme.desc: We look back on the extraordinary career of Mohamed Salah, tracing his rise from a small village in Egypt to global football stardom. We speak to those closest to him to highlight his early years, struggles, and determination, shedding light on the pivotal moments that shaped his journey to becoming a Premier League legend with Liverpool.

programme.title: REVIEW OF THE SEASON 2019/2020 (LIV)
programme.sub-title: A review of all the best goals, action and drama from Premier League seasons past.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP41: MORATO
programme.sub-title: “Beautiful!” was the reaction from Morato when he first set eyes on The City Ground. Having joined Nottingham Forest last summer, the Brazilian sits down with The Big Interview in the final week of what’s been a remarkable season. The 23-year-old shares his thoughts about Forest’s fortunes under Nuno Espirito Santo, his career journey to England and how losing his sister to cancer when she was just 18 has impacted his life.
programme.desc: “Beautiful!” was the reaction from Morato when he first set eyes on The City Ground. programme.title: PL STORIES EP51: GOODBYE GOODISON
programme.sub-title: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.
programme.desc: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP50: CHRIS WOOD
programme.sub-title: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he’s become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.
programme.desc: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. programme.title: PL GOATS EP05: AMERICAN STARS
programme.sub-title: Premier League legend Tim Howard takes on the ultimate challenge, ranking the 10 greatest US players to play in the Premier League. But there’s a twist; from his carefully selected list, he must crown one as the Ultimate Premier League USA GOAT.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP20: MCI v QPR 11/12
programme.sub-title: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.
programme.desc: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season.
